SourceTracker Decontamination

Gabriela Puerres

2025-07-07

Contaminant Identification with SourceTracker

R package ‘SourceTracker’ version 1.0.

1. Setting

Load Required Libraries

# SourceTracker Libraries
library (dplyr)
library (phyloseq)
library (vegan)
library (ggplot2)
library (MicrobiotaProcess)
library (tidyr)
library (data.table)
library(devtools)
library (pairwiseAdonis)
library (microbiome)
library (patchwork)

# Paths
path.rds      <- "~/Cow_Inflammation_Study/Decontamination/Analysis/RDS"
path.figures  <- "~/Cow_Inflammation_Study/Decontamination/Analysis/Figures"
path.results  <- "~/Cow_Inflammation_Study/Decontamination/Analysis/Results"

source("~/Cow_Inflammation_Study/Decontamination/Scripts/SourceTracker.r") # Load SourceTracker package

Assign control as source, samples as sink:

SourceTracker with Biome Samples (“Feces”) as Sink Environments.

Sink:

  • “Feces”
  • “Mock_A”
  • “Mock_B”

Source:

  • “Reagent_Control”
  • “Extraction_Control”
  • “Environmental_Control”

Load Phyloseq object and Metadata

# Load data
#Phyloseq Object after Decontam
ps.decontam <- readRDS(file.path(path.rds,"ps.decontam.rds"))
ps.decontam
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 21818 taxa and 162 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 162 samples by 68 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 21818 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# Metadata / Label samples as source or sink
metadata <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps.decontam))
metadata$SourceSink <- NA
metadata$SourceSink[ metadata$Sample_Type %in% 
    c("Reagent_Control", "Extraction_Control", "Environmental_Control") ] <- "source"
metadata$SourceSink[ metadata$Sample_Type %in% 
    c("Feces", "Mock_A", "Mock_B") ] <- "sink"
metadata$SourceSink
##   [1] "sink"   "sink"   "source" "sink"   "sink"   "source" "sink"   "sink"  
##   [9] "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"  
##  [17] "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"  
##  [25] "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"  
##  [33] "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "source" "sink"  
##  [41] "sink"   "source" "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"  
##  [49] "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"  
##  [57] "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"  
##  [65] "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"  
##  [73] "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"  
##  [81] "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"  
##  [89] "source" "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"  
##  [97] "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"  
## [105] "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"  
## [113] "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"  
## [121] "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"  
## [129] "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"  
## [137] "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "sink"  
## [145] "sink"   "sink"   "sink"   "source" "source" "source" "source" "source"
## [153] "source" "source" "source" "source" "source" "sink"   "sink"   "sink"  
## [161] "sink"   "sink"
# Prepare OTU matrix
# Sourcetracker wants the sample names as rows in an otu table and the metadatafile, which is the opposite of how phyloseq likes it!
otus <- as.matrix(data.frame(otu_table(ps.decontam)))
dim(otus) # 21818 (OTUs),   162 (Samples)
## [1]   162 21818
# Extract only those samples in common between the two tables (metadata and otus):
common.sample.ids  <- intersect(rownames(metadata),rownames(otus))
st_otus <- otus[common.sample.ids , , drop = FALSE]
metadata <- metadata[common.sample.ids , , drop = FALSE]


# Double-check that the mapping file and otu table had overlapping samples:
if(length(common.sample.ids) <= 1) {
 message <- paste(sprintf('Error: there are %d sample ids in
 common '),'between the metadata file
 and data table')
 stop(message)
}

2. Train the model

# Extract the source environments and source/sink indices
train.ix <- which(metadata$SourceSink=='source')
test.ix <- which(metadata$SourceSink=='sink')


# Check sink OTU table dimensions:
dim(otus[test.ix, ]) # 147 21818
## [1]   147 21818
length(test.ix) # 147  
## [1] 147
# env
envs <- metadata$Sample_Type
envs
##   [1] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Environmental_Control"
##   [4] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Environmental_Control"
##   [7] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [10] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [13] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [16] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [19] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [22] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [25] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [28] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [31] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [34] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [37] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Environmental_Control"
##  [40] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Environmental_Control"
##  [43] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [46] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [49] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [52] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [55] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [58] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [61] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [64] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [67] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [70] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [73] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [76] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [79] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [82] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [85] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [88] "Feces"                 "Environmental_Control" "Feces"                
##  [91] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [94] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
##  [97] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
## [100] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
## [103] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
## [106] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
## [109] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
## [112] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
## [115] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
## [118] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
## [121] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
## [124] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
## [127] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
## [130] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
## [133] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
## [136] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
## [139] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
## [142] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
## [145] "Feces"                 "Feces"                 "Feces"                
## [148] "Extraction_Control"    "Extraction_Control"    "Extraction_Control"   
## [151] "Extraction_Control"    "Extraction_Control"    "Reagent_Control"      
## [154] "Reagent_Control"       "Reagent_Control"       "Reagent_Control"      
## [157] "Reagent_Control"       "Mock_A"                "Mock_A"               
## [160] "Mock_A"                "Mock_B"                "Mock_B"
if(is.element('Cow_ID',colnames(metadata))) desc <- metadata$Cow_ID
#Tune the alpha values using cross-validation (this is slow!)
alpha1 <- alpha2 <- 0.001
alpha1 # 0.001
## [1] 0.001
alpha2 # 0.001
## [1] 0.001
# Train SourceTracker object on training data
st <- sourcetracker(otus[train.ix,],  envs[train.ix], rarefaction_depth = NULL)  # Skip rarefaction
#Export Data
otu_train <-otus[train.ix,]
otu_envs <-envs[train.ix]

saveRDS(st, (file.path(path.rds, "st.rds")))
saveRDS(otu_train, (file.path(path.rds, "otu_train.rds")))
saveRDS(otu_envs, (file.path(path.rds, "otu_envs.rds")))
saveRDS(envs, (file.path(path.rds, "envs.rds")))
saveRDS(train.ix, (file.path(path.rds, "train.ix.rds")))
saveRDS(test.ix, (file.path(path.rds, "test.ix.rds")))

3. Prediction

# Define auto rarefaction depth based on training sample read counts
rarefaction_auto <- min(rowSums(otus[train.ix, ]))
rarefaction <- rarefaction_auto


# Predict source proportions in parallel
#results2 <- predict(st,otus[test.ix,], 
#                   alpha1=alpha1, 
#                   alpha2=alpha2,
#                   full.results = TRUE,
#                   rarefaction_depth =rarefaction)

# Save the results
#saveRDS(results, file.path(path.rds, "sourcetracker_results2.rds"))

The RDS file generated after prediction must be of class sourcetracker.fit

results <- readRDS("~/Cow_Inflammation_Study/Decontamination/Analysis/RDS/sourcetracker_results2.rds")
class(results)
## [1] "sourcetracker.fit"

Check the prediction:

# Check results output
class(results)        #  "sourcetracker.fit"
## [1] "sourcetracker.fit"
dim(results$draws)   # 10 x 4 x 147 = taxa × envs × samples
## [1]  10   4 147
nrow(results$proportions)  # 147
## [1] 147
# For each sink sample (147), there's 10 draws of predicted proportions across 4 sources.
#draws: [10 × 4 × 147] = shape (taxa × envs × samples=1)
#proportions:[147 × 4] = shape (samples × envs)
#proportions_sd:[147 × 4] = shape (samples × envs)
#train.envs: [4]
#samplenames: [147] 
#full.results: [10 × 4 × 21818   × 147] = shape (taxa × envs × features × samples)

prop <- results$proportions   # samples × sources
contaminants_proportion_table <- as.data.frame(results$proportions)
write.csv(contaminants_proportion_table,
          file = file.path(path.results, "contaminants_proportion_table.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE,
          quote = FALSE)

# Now, check the contaminants information:
# Samples with any extraction contribution
extraction_pos <- rownames(prop)[ prop[,"Extraction_Control"] > 0.00 ]
str(extraction_pos)
##  chr [1:147] "1165D1LM" "1165D3LM" "1179D1LM" "1179D3LM" "1217D1LM" ...
# Samples with any environmental contribution
environmental_pos <- rownames(prop)[ prop[,"Environmental_Control"] > 0 ]
str(environmental_pos)
##  chr [1:27] "1234D1LM" "1308D3LM" "1349D1LM" "1355D3LM" "1354D3LM" ...
# Samples with any reagent contribution
reagent_pos <- rownames(prop)[ prop[,"Reagent_Control"] > 0 ]
str(reagent_pos)
##  chr [1:26] "1234D1LM" "1308D3LM" "1314D3LM" "1349D1LM" "1409D1LM" ...
contaminant_sources <- list(
    extraction_pos       = extraction_pos,
  environmental_pos    = environmental_pos,
  reagent_pos          = reagent_pos
)

# Identify the ASVs that are contaminants:
contam_indices <- grep("Control", results$train.envs)
log_contam <- results$full.results[, contam_indices, , ] > 0
is_contaminant_asv <- apply(log_contam, MARGIN = 3, FUN = any)
asv_ids <- colnames(otus)
names(is_contaminant_asv) <- asv_ids
contaminant_asvs <- asv_ids[is_contaminant_asv] # vector of contaminant ASV names
cat(sprintf("Found %d contaminant ASVs.\n", length(contaminant_asvs)))
## Found 91 contaminant ASVs.
# Create directory to store outcomes
base_dir <- "~/Cow_Inflammation_Study/Decontamination/Analysis/Results"
outdir <- "Results_sourcetracker_cow"
filebase <- "ST_Cow_samples"
subdir <- file.path(base_dir, outdir, "full_results")
dir.create(subdir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Mean and Sample depths
res.mean <- apply(results$full.results,c(2,3,4),mean)  # Average of full results across all runs
sample.depths <- apply(results$full.results[1,,,,drop=F],4,sum) # Depth of each sample for relative abundance 

# write each environment as a separate file
for(i in 1:length(results$train.envs)){
  env.name <- results$train.envs[i]
  filename.fractions <- sprintf('%s/%s_%s_contributions.txt', subdir, filebase, env.name)
  res.mean.i <- res.mean[i,,]
  # handle the case where there is only one sink sample
  if(is.null(dim(res.mean.i))) res.mean.i <- matrix(res.mean.i,ncol=1)
  
  # make rows be samples, columns be features
  res.mean.i <- t(res.mean.i)
  
  # ensure proper names are retained
  colnames(res.mean.i) <- colnames(otus)[1:ncol(res.mean.i)]
  rownames(res.mean.i) <- results$samplenames
  
  # calculate and save relative abundance
  res.mean.i.ra <- sweep(res.mean.i,1,sample.depths,'/')
  sink(filename.fractions)
  cat('SampleID\t')
  write.table(res.mean.i.ra,quote=F,sep='\t')
  sink(NULL)
}
# Plot types
if(dim(results$draws)[2] > 1) {
  plot.types <- c('pie')
}

# Extract sample‑type labels
meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps.decontam))
envs<-meta[rownames(results$proportions),'Sample_Type']
envs<-as.factor(unlist(envs))
labels = sprintf('%s_%s',envs, rownames(results$proportions))
plotixs <- sort(as.numeric(envs),index=TRUE)$ix


# Plot
for (plot.type in plot.types) {   # plot each environment separately
  for (env in levels(envs)) {
    plotixs <- which(envs == env)
    out_png  <- file.path(outdir,
                          sprintf("%s_%s_%s.png",
                                  filebase,
                                  plot.type,
                                  env))
    png(out_png, width = 500, height = 500)
    plot(results,
         type           = plot.type,
         labels         = labels,
         include.legend = TRUE,
         indices        = plotixs)
    dev.off()
  }
}

4. Check SourceTracker object

# read phyloseq object after decontam
ps.decontam <- readRDS(file.path(path.rds, "ps.decontam.rds"))
ps.decontam
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 21818 taxa and 162 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 162 samples by 68 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 21818 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
target <- c("Feces", "Mock_A", "Mock_B")
ps.sink <- subset_samples(ps.decontam, Sample_Type %in% target)
ps.sink
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 21818 taxa and 147 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 147 samples by 68 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 21818 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]

5. Remove contaminants from positive samples (“Feces”, “Mock_A”, “Mock_B”) using negative controls as source

# Read in all four contributions, keep only sink samples:
path.contaminants <- file.path(path.results, "Results_sourcetracker_cow/full_results")

env_files <- list(
  Environmental_Control = "ST_Cow_samples_Environmental_Control_contributions.txt",
  Extraction_Control   = "ST_Cow_samples_Extraction_Control_contributions.txt",
  Reagent_Control      = "ST_Cow_samples_Reagent_Control_contributions.txt",
  Unknown              = "ST_Cow_samples_Unknown_contributions.txt"
)

contam_list <- lapply(env_files, function(fn) {
  df <- fread(file.path(path.contaminants, fn)) %>% as.data.frame()
  rownames(df) <- df[[1]]
  df[[1]] <- NULL
  df
})

6. Create Phyloseq object

a. Create Phyloseq object for contaminants identified by SourceTracker

# Sample depths 
sample.depths <- sample_sums(ps.sink) # Total reads per sample
length(sample.depths)  
## [1] 147
# Create the otu table for contaminants by multiplying sample depths. From contaminant fractions to contaminant counts
contam_sum <- Reduce(`+`, contam_list[c("Environmental_Control",
                                        "Extraction_Control",
                                        "Reagent_Control")])
dim(contam_sum)
## [1]   147 21818
otu_contam <- sweep(contam_sum, 1, sample.depths, `*`) %>% round() 
dim(otu_contam) 
## [1]   147 21818
rowSums(otu_contam)
##              1165D1LM              1165D3LM              1179D1LM 
##                 33438                 44672                 33578 
##              1179D3LM              1217D1LM              1217D3LM 
##                 44972                 60123                 37700 
##              1218D1LM              1218D3LM              1226D1LM 
##                 42040                 37665                 34649 
##              1226D3LM              1228D1LM              1228D3LM 
##                 38234                 30953                 52775 
##              1234D1LM              1234D3LM              1261D1LM 
##                 38724                 51055                 35357 
##              1261D3LM              1287D1LM              1287D3LM 
##                 46396                 44254                 63748 
##              1308D1LM              1308D3LM              1314D1LM 
##                 39066                 37186                 21749 
##              1314D3LM              1320D1LM              1320D3LM 
##                 52378                 48279                 42581 
##              1349D1LM              1349D3LM              1355D1LM 
##                 57330                 47157                 68260 
##              1355D3LM              1354D1LM              1354D3LM

##              1368D1LM              1368D3LM              1409D1LM 
##                 53815                 28710                 54931 
##              1409D3LM               279D1LM               279D3LM 
##                106311                 56532                 43403 
##               288D1LM               288D3LM               302D1LM 
##                 51808                 78125                 44314 
##               302D3LM               321D1LM               321D3LM

##                  4349                 21540                 55143

##               519D1LM               519D3LM               580D1LM 
##                 35150                 38131                 51945 
##               580D3LM              5839D1LM              5839D3LM 
##                 52782                  9487                 30525

##              5943D3LM              5967D1LM              5967D3LM

##               608D1LM               608D3LM               616D1LM 
##                 46045                 86954                 76408 
##               616D3LM              6174D1LM              6174D3LM 
##                 43945                 59593                 62675 
##              6197D1LM              6197D3LM              6209D1LM 
##                 11494                 19479                 38365 
##              6209D3LM              6333D1LM              6333D3LM 
##                 61937                   951                 73314 
##              6347D1LM              6347D3LM               652D1LM 
##                 26794                 49176                 60222 
##               652D3LM               657D1LM               657D3LM 
##                 81423                 56242                 43782 
##              6767D1LM              6767D3LM               694D1LM 
##                 94022                 60366                 53590 
##               694D3LM              6970D1LM              6970D3LM 
##                 33031                 44791                 39425 
##              6990D1LM              6990D3LM               699D1LM 
##                 56415                 57646                 43224 
##               699D3LM              7004D1LM              7004D3LM 
##                 38135                 54807                 36828 
##               703D1LM               703D3LM              7040D1LM 
##                 49398                 73791                 58437 
##              7040D3LM              7046D1LM              7046D3LM 
##                 50234                 45882                 31119 
##              7093D1LM              7093D3LM              7095D1LM 
##                 23326                 36478                 82332 
##              7095D3LM              7101D1LM              7101D3LM 
##                 68504                 61070                 45879 
##               720D1LM               720D3LM              7239D1LM 
##                 44551                 38818                 41083 
##              7239D3LM              7267D1LM              7267D3LM 
##                 38677                 38345                 14615 
##              7346D1LM              7346D3LM              7366D1LM 
##                 27576                 46368                 51284 
##              7366D3LM               742D1LM               742D3LM 
##                 75864                 66258                 43890 
##               752D1LM               752D3LM               754D1LM 
##                 66482                 57068                 34783 
##               754D3LM               756D1LM               756D3LM 
##                 57015                 30321                 44537 
##               762D1LM               762D3LM               773D1LM 
##                 47271                 81220                 64228 
##               773D3LM              8149D1LM              8149D3LM 
##                 80449                 34339                 39727 
##              8560D1LM              8560D3LM              8644D1LM 
##                 57817                 56019                 26731 
##              8644D3LM              9093D1LM              9093D3LM 
##                 62622                 60269                 53260 
##              9539D1LM              9539D3LM              9967D1LM 
##                 61344                 58677                  2421 
##              9967D3LM mock-synthetic-ZYMO-1 mock-synthetic-ZYMO-2 
##                 35857                  2739                  3225 
## mock-synthetic-ZYMO-3 mock-synthetic-ZYMO-4 mock-synthetic-ZYMO-5 
##                  6368                   471                   144
# Keep only ASVs with >0 contaminant reads
contam_in_table <- intersect(colnames(otu_contam), contaminant_asvs)
otu_contam_filt <- otu_contam[, contam_in_table, drop = FALSE]
cat("Dimensions of filtered table:", dim(otu_contam_filt), "\n")
## Dimensions of filtered table: 147 91
# Save the final ASV list
saveRDS(contam_in_table, file = file.path(path.rds, "SourceTracker_contaminant_ASVs.rds"))
# Phyloseq object for Contaminants found by SourceTracker
ps.st.contam.only <- prune_taxa(colnames(otu_contam_filt), ps.sink)
ps.st.contam.only
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 91 taxa and 147 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 147 samples by 68 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 91 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
otu_table(ps.st.contam.only) <- otu_table(otu_contam_filt, taxa_are_rows = FALSE)
dim(otu_table(ps.st.contam.only))
## [1] 147  91
dim(otu_contam_filt)
## [1] 147  91
otu_table <- as.data.frame(otu_table(ps.st.contam.only))
dim(otu_table)
## [1] 147  91
# Save the rds file
saveRDS(ps.st.contam.only, file.path(path.rds,"ps.st.contam.only.rds"))

b. Create Phyloseq object for non-contaminants after SourceTracker

# Sample depths 
sample.depths <- sample_sums(ps.sink) # Total reads per sample
length(sample.depths)  
## [1] 147
# Create the otu table for contaminants by multiplying sample depths. From contaminant fractions to contaminant counts
decontam_sum <- Reduce(`+`, contam_list[c("Unknown")])
dim(decontam_sum)
## [1]   147 21818
otu_noncontam <- sweep(decontam_sum, 1, sample.depths, `*`) %>% round() 
dim(otu_noncontam) 
## [1]   147 21818
rowSums(otu_noncontam)
##              1165D1LM              1165D3LM              1179D1LM 
##                336315                352632                358504 
##              1179D3LM              1217D1LM              1217D3LM

##               720D1LM               720D3LM              7239D1LM 
##                390549                399702                330557 
##              7239D3LM              7267D1LM              7267D3LM

##              7346D1LM              7346D3LM              7366D1LM 
##                218660                341293                504042 
##              7366D3LM               742D1LM               742D3LM 
##                278015                505978                419415 
##               752D1LM               752D3LM               754D1LM 
##                568007                352790                282685 
##               754D3LM               756D1LM               756D3LM 
##                260718                319787                314888 
##               762D1LM               762D3LM               773D1LM 
##                371354                475347                361922 
##               773D3LM              8149D1LM              8149D3LM 
##                285887                271043                419020 
##              8560D1LM              8560D3LM              8644D1LM 
##                367692                273783                353268 
##              8644D3LM              9093D1LM              9093D3LM 
##                367915                369641                281994 
##              9539D1LM              9539D3LM              9967D1LM 
##                445451                420358                 11317 
##              9967D3LM mock-synthetic-ZYMO-1 mock-synthetic-ZYMO-2 
##                281654                633390                670800 
## mock-synthetic-ZYMO-3 mock-synthetic-ZYMO-4 mock-synthetic-ZYMO-5 
##                733066                 40540                 10229
all_ASVs  <- colnames(otu_noncontam)
noncontaminant_ASVs <- setdiff(all_ASVs, contaminant_asvs)
otu_noncontam_filt <- otu_noncontam[, noncontaminant_ASVs, drop = FALSE]
dim(otu_noncontam_filt)
## [1]   147 21727
seq_noncontam <- otu_table(otu_noncontam_filt, taxa_are_rows = FALSE)

#Meta and Taxa
meta_noncontam <- sample_data(ps.sink)
tax_noncontam <- phyloseq::tax_table(ps.sink)

# Phyloseq Object for non contaminants
ps.st.decontam <- phyloseq(seq_noncontam, tax_noncontam, meta_noncontam)
ps.st.decontam
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 21727 taxa and 147 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 147 samples by 68 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 21727 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# Save the rds file
saveRDS(ps.st.decontam, file.path(path.rds,"ps.st.decontam.rds"))

7. Plots

a. Plot proportion of contaminants

meta <- sample_data(ps.sink) %>% data.frame()

# proportion of contaminants is stored at results$proportions

prop.type <- results$proportions %>% data.frame()
prop.type$Sample_Type <- meta$Sample_Type
prop.type.long <- prop.type %>% pivot_longer(-Sample_Type, names_to = "Source", values_to = "Proportion")

prop <- results$proportions %>% data.frame()
prop$Sample_ID <- meta$Corrected_Sample_ID 

prop.long <- prop %>% pivot_longer(-Sample_ID,names_to="Source",values_to="Proportion")
prop.long$Sample_Type <- prop.type.long$Sample_Type

type_colors <-
  c(
    "Extraction_Control" = "red",
    "Environmental_Control" = "orange",
    "Reagent_Control" = "skyblue",
    "Unknown" = "lightgreen"
  )

p.prop.contam <- ggplot(data=prop.long, mapping = aes(x = Sample_ID, y = Proportion, fill = Source))+
  geom_bar(stat="identity") +
  facet_wrap(~Sample_Type, nrow = 1, scales = "free_x") +
  theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x =  element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 7),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank())+
  scale_fill_manual(values = type_colors)
print(p.prop.contam)

b. Plot proportion of contaminants 2: Expanding upper segment

# install.packages("ggbreak") 
library(ggbreak)
p.prop.contam.2 <- ggplot(data=prop.long, aes(x = Sample_ID, y = Proportion, fill = Source)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity") +
  facet_wrap(~Sample_Type, nrow = 1, scales = "free_x") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x =  element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 7),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = type_colors) +
  scale_y_break(c(0.75, 0.75), scales = 0.5)  # adjust the range and scale factor as needed
print(p.prop.contam.2)

c. Plot abundance of contaminants

ps.st.contam.only
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 91 taxa and 147 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 147 samples by 68 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 91 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
detection <- sum(taxa_sums(ps.st.contam.only))/147

# genera with <5% relative abundance and <25% prevalence were grouped to Others
pseq.contam <- aggregate_rare(ps.st.contam.only, level="Genus", detection = detection*0.05, prevalence = 0.25)

melt.pseq.contam <- psmelt(pseq.contam)
p.contam <-  ggplot(melt.pseq.contam, aes(x = Corrected_Sample_ID, y = Abundance, fill = Genus)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity") +
  facet_wrap(~Sample_Type, nrow = 1, scales = "free_x") +
   labs(
    title = "Abundance of Contaminants",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Abundance"
  ) +
  theme_bw()+
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x =  element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 7),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    legend.key.size = unit(10, "pt"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )+
  scale_fill_brewer("Genus", palette = "Paired")
p.contam

d. Composition True Samples

ps.st.decontam
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 21727 taxa and 147 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 147 samples by 68 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 21727 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
detection <- sum(taxa_sums(ps.st.decontam))/147

# genera with <5% relative abundance and <25% prevalence were grouped to Others
pseq.decontam <- aggregate_rare(ps.st.decontam, level="Genus", detection = detection*0.05, prevalence = 0.25)

#plot
melt.pseq.decontam <- psmelt(pseq.decontam)
p.decontam <-  ggplot(melt.pseq.decontam, aes(x = Corrected_Sample_ID, y = Abundance, fill = Genus)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity") +
  facet_wrap(~Sample_Type, nrow = 1, scales = "free_x") +
   labs(
    title = "Composition True Samples",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Abundance"
  ) +
  theme_bw()+
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x =  element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 7),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    legend.key.size = unit(10, "pt"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )+
  scale_fill_brewer("Genus", palette = "Paired")
print(p.decontam)

e. Merge plots

p.contam.st <- ((p.prop.contam.2) / (p.contam) /(p.decontam)) + plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")
print(p.contam.st)

ggsave(file.path(path.figures, "Figure 4. sourcetracker_contaminants_per_sample.png"), p.contam.st, width = 12, height = 10, dpi=600)
